title: Cheap Houses for Rent in Glenville, Cleveland
metaTitle: Cheap Houses for Rent in Glenville, Cleveland OH
metaDescription: Cheap houses for rent in Glenville, Cleveland (44108) — budget 1-3BR homes near University Circle from a local, voucher-friendly rental team.
primaryKeyword: cheap houses for rent Glenville cleveland
answerBox: Glenville, in Cleveland's ZIP code 44108, offers rental houses below the citywide median. One-bedroom units can start around $700/month, two-bedroom homes around $800, and every home we work with in the area welcomes Housing Choice Vouchers.

sections.h2: Finding a cheap house to rent in Glenville
body: Glenville sits in **ZIP code 44108** on Cleveland's northeast side, bordered by University Circle to the south and crossed by St. Clair Avenue's commercial strip. Its housing stock is dominated by early-1900s two- and three-story homes, many split into duplex units, which keeps a good share of listings priced below what newer apartment buildings charge elsewhere in the city.

We work with voucher-friendly rental homes throughout Glenville as part of our broader concentration of properties on Cleveland's northeast side. [Tell us your budget](/p/book-showing) and we'll follow up with homes that currently fit it.

sections.h2: What a budget rental looks like in Glenville
body: On the lower end of the market, one-bedroom units — mostly upper floors of converted duplexes — can be found from roughly $700 a month, with two-bedroom homes generally starting around $800. Three-bedroom houses run higher, typically $1,050 to $1,300, but even that upper range sits close to Cleveland's citywide median rather than above it.

Zumper's July 4, 2026 report puts Cleveland's overall median asking rent at **$1,250/month**, with 2-bedrooms at $1,100 and 3-bedrooms at $1,350 — Glenville's budget-tier listings generally price at or below those citywide figures. RentCafe's July 2, 2026 report on larger, professionally-managed apartment buildings shows a much higher citywide average of $1,818 for a 2-bedroom, which reflects a different segment of the market than Glenville's individually-owned rental houses.

rows: Mostly upper units in converted duplexes
Lower end of the typical range

sections.h2: Stretching your rental budget in Glenville
body: Because most Glenville rentals are older, individually-owned homes rather than large managed complexes, whether utilities are included in the rent can shift the real monthly cost significantly — always confirm this before comparing two listings on price alone. Some units also include off-street parking or a garage, which can offset the cost of maintaining a car in a neighborhood well served by transit.

RTA bus routes run along St. Clair Avenue and Superior Avenue, both connecting to the HealthLine bus rapid transit corridor on Euclid Avenue, which links Public Square downtown to University Circle without a car. Rockefeller Park and its Cultural Gardens run along the neighborhood's western edge, offering free outdoor space close to home.

It's also worth asking each landlord directly about lease length and move-in costs — a lower monthly rent can still come with a security deposit and last month's rent due upfront, so the total move-in cost matters as much as the advertised monthly figure when comparing budget listings against each other.

items: **Transit:** RTA bus routes on St. Clair and Superior Avenues; near the HealthLine and University Circle
**Highway access:** I-90 a short trip north

sections.h2: Section 8 and voucher-friendly homes in Glenville
body: A Housing Choice Voucher can make an already affordable Glenville rental even more manageable — every home we work with in the area accepts Section 8 and is HUD-inspection-ready. Ohio has no statewide source-of-income protection law, and the City of Cleveland does not currently require landlords to accept vouchers; only Cleveland Heights, South Euclid, University Heights, Warrensville Heights, and Linndale have local ordinances doing so. Apply through [CMHA](https://www.cmha.net) and see our [Cleveland Section 8 guide](/section-8-housing-cleveland-oh/) for the full process, or read our [full Glenville neighborhood guide](/cleveland-rentals/neighborhoods/houses-for-rent-glenville-cleveland/) for more on the area.

Whether you're using a voucher or paying rent on your own, the same standard screening applies to every applicant on every budget-tier listing — we don't offer a way to skip income or background verification. [Book a showing](/p/book-showing) to see a specific home in person, then start your application online through our secure DoorLoop portal once you're ready to move forward.

faq.q: What's the cheapest rent for a house in Glenville?
faq.a: One-bedroom units in Glenville can start around $700 a month, with two-bedroom homes generally starting near $800. Three-bedroom houses run higher, typically $1,050 to $1,300. Exact pricing depends on condition, size, and whether utilities are included.

faq.q: Why are rentals in Glenville cheaper than some other Cleveland neighborhoods?
faq.a: Glenville's rental stock is mostly early-1900s duplexes and single-family homes rather than newer apartment buildings, which keeps asking rents at or below Cleveland's citywide median of $1,250 reported by Zumper in July 2026.

faq.q: Are there Section 8 houses for rent in Glenville?
faq.a: Yes. Every home we work with in Glenville accepts Housing Choice Vouchers and is HUD-inspection-ready. Book a showing to see what's currently available in the area.

faq.q: What ZIP code is Glenville in?
faq.a: Glenville is in ZIP code 44108, on Cleveland's northeast side, bordering University Circle to the south with St. Clair Avenue running through the neighborhood's commercial corridor.

faq.q: Can I get to University Circle without a car from a cheap rental in Glenville?
faq.a: Yes. Glenville is served by RTA bus routes on St. Clair and Superior Avenues that connect to the HealthLine bus rapid transit corridor on Euclid Avenue, which runs directly to University Circle and downtown, so a car isn't required for that commute.
